title: Mentoring Programs * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Mentoring Programs * Homeless Youth 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disabilities Issues Homeless Youth LGBTQ2+ Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to music lessons, art workshops, recording studio sessions, roleplaying game sessions, cultural outings, meals, hygiene supplies, clothing, first aid supplies, and mentorship services. 16 NW Broadway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Homeless youth ages 15-25 Hours: Monday-Thursday 4pm-8pm, Friday by appointment only Email (503) 481-4970 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER HEARTS WITH A MISSION YOUTH SHELTER P:EAR P:EAR
